info.title: Tempera Workflows API
info.description: Deterministic workflow engine for the Tempera ecosystem: bounded-DAG workflows (tempera.workflow/v1) of typed nodes — native orchestration nodes plus sdk.<product>.<opId> nodes generated from the tempera-sdk surface — executed as replayable, event-streamed runs. Thin orchestrator: code runs in cradle, LLM calls go through tempera-llm, data operations through data-engine.

get.summary: The studio palette: every node type with config JSON Schema and outputs.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Opaque continuation token returned by the preceding list response.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Opaque continuation token returned by the preceding list response.

get.summary: Full run view: status, per-node states with outputs/logs/metrics, timestamps.
Server-sent events for a run: replays history from `?since=seq`, then
get.description:
streams live `run.state` / `node.state` / `node.log` events until the run
reaches a terminal state. SSE `id` = seq, `event` = type.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Replay events with seq greater than this (default 0 = from the start).

post.summary: Cancel a queued/running run. Already-cancelled runs return 200

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Opaque continuation token returned by the preceding list response.

post.summary: Create a workflow from a `tempera.workflow/v1` definition.

delete.summary: Delete a workflow. Existing runs keep their definition snapshots.

patch.summary: Replace a workflow definition through an AIP-style masked update.

parameters.name: updateMask
parameters.description: Fields to replace. This full-definition endpoint accepts `definition` or `*`.

post.summary: Start a run (job pattern): returns immediately with the queued run; poll
post.description:
`runs.get` or stream `runs.events`. `idempotencyKey` replays are returned
with 200 instead of 202.

200.description: Idempotent replay of an existing run

post.summary: Create a run AND wait for it to finish, returning the run + output in one
post.description:
response — the customer-facing "one call, several services under the hood"
surface. Reuses the exact run machinery as `runs.create` (same executor),
then waits up to `min(waitMs, settings.deadlineMs)` (default `waitMs` = 30s,
well under proxy idle limits). If the run has not
finished by then it returns 200 with the still-running run and a poll hint;
the run is never orphaned — it keeps executing in the background.

200.description: The run finished (or the wait bound elapsed with status=running)

post.summary: Ask Tempera Code to propose a validated workflow draft, or search the full
post.description:
node catalog when the agent is not connected. This method never saves or
runs the returned definition. A supplied draft may use a fresh id that is
not stored yet, which powers the Studio's prompt-first creation flow;
applying it still requires an explicit create/update action.

200.description: Validated draft proposal or full-catalog node matches

404.description: Workflow not found and no draft supplied

post.summary: Generate or repair one JSON configuration value with the configured
post.description:
server-side workflow model. The result is parsed and shape-validated before
it is returned; this method never saves a workflow or executes a node.

400.description: Invalid prompt, mode, or editor value

503.description: Workflow model unavailable or returned the wrong shape

post.summary: Validate a definition without saving it. Returns 200 with the issue list
post.description: even when invalid — this is the studio's live linting endpoint.

400.description: Body is not a workflow definition

output.description:
For multi-output nodes (`branch.condition`): the route name
(`true`/`false`). For single-output nodes: an optional selector into the
output — a JSON pointer when it starts with `/`, else an object key.

input.description:
Key under which the value lands in the target's input object. When
omitted, object values are shallow-merged and scalars land under the
source node id.

message.description: Human-readable description safe to show to operators.

retryable.description: Whether retrying the identical request may succeed.

status.description: HTTP status carried in the body for stream/proxy contexts.

ErrorResponse.description: Wire envelope for every non-2xx response.

JsonAssistMode.description:
Whether JSON assist creates a value from a description or repairs the
current editor text.

JsonAssistPurpose.description: Semantic rules applied after the model returns JSON.

JsonAssistRequest.description:
Generate or repair one JSON editor value. This authoring helper never
saves a workflow or executes a node.

context.description: Human-readable field context such as "Templates" or "JSON schema".

current.description: Raw editor text. Required in repair mode so malformed JSON can be fixed.

prompt.description:
Natural-language description, for example "a nested customer object
with a required string id and an optional array of labels".

JsonAssistResponse.description: Strictly parsed and server-validated JSON returned to an editor.

JsonRootKind.description: Required root shape for the generated JSON value.

config.description: Type-specific configuration, validated against the registry JSON Schema.

id.description: Graph-unique id (`[A-Za-z0-9_.-]{1,64}`); the deterministic tie-break key.

name.description: Optional display name for the studio canvas.

pinnedOutput.description:
Pinned test output. When a run is created with `usePinned: true`, this
value is returned verbatim without executing the node (studio testing).

type.description: Node type from the registry (`GET /v1/node-types`), e.g. `python.execute`.

NodeRole.description: Semantic role rendered for a node in the workflow graph.

NodeState.description: Recorded state of one node within a run.

output.description:
Node output, possibly truncated to the run's `maxOutputBytes` — a
truncated value is replaced by `{"truncated":true,"bytes":N,"preview":"..."}`.

NodeSuggestion.description: One relevant node from the complete native + SDK catalog.

NodeTypeInfo.description: One node type in the studio palette catalog.

apiPath.description: Upstream resource or custom-method path for an SDK action.

category.description: trigger | compute | control | llm | output | sdk.

httpMethod.description: Upstream HTTP method for an SDK action.

operationId.description: SDK operation id without the `sdk.<product>.` prefix.

requiredInputs.description: Inputs that must be supplied by configuration or incoming node data.

type.description: Unique type name used in workflow definitions.

verified.description:
Honesty flag, present where the operation was cross-checked against the
upstream product's real contract (today: dataEngine). `false` marks a
phantom SDK operation — the studio should badge it as unverified.

NodeTypeList.description: `GET /v1/node-types` response: the palette catalog, grouped by product.

groups.description: Group index: category or `sdk.<product>` -> node type names.

OnErrorMode.description: How the engine reacts to a node failure.

OnErrorPolicy.description: Per-node failure policy, attached to a [`NodeDef`] as `onError`.

RetryPolicy.description: Bounded retry policy for a node.

backoffMs.description: Fixed delay between attempts in ms (0..=[`MAX_RETRY_BACKOFF_MS`]).

output.description: Outputs of the workflow's sink nodes, keyed by node id (size-capped).

idempotencyKey.description:
Replay-safe key: an identical `(workflow, idempotencyKey)` returns the
existing run instead of starting a new one.

input.description: Payload delivered to the trigger node (default `{}`).

only.description:
Execute exactly this set of node ids; all others are skipped. Combine
with `seedOutputs` for a "test single node" run.

seedOutputs.description:
Inject outputs for nodes treated as already-produced, so `startAt`/`only`
have upstream data. Keyed by node id.

startAt.description:
Execute only this node and its transitive descendants; ancestors are
skipped unless supplied via `seedOutputs`.

usePinned.description:
When true, any node with `pinnedOutput` set returns it verbatim without
executing (studio testing of downstream logic). Default false.

RunEvent.description:
One replayable run event (also the SSE `data` payload; the SSE `id` is
`seq` and the SSE `event` is `type`).

seq.description: Monotonic per-run sequence, starting at 1. Resume with `?since=seq`.

Severity.description:
Severity of a [`ValidationIssue`]. Defaults to `error` for backward
compatibility: an issue deserialized without a severity is an error, and
`runs.create` refuses only on `error`-severity issues.

nodeId.description: Offending node id when the issue is node-scoped.

WorkflowAttachment.description:
A small, ephemeral attachment used while composing. Attachments are never
persisted in the workflow definition or returned to the browser.

WorkflowAttachmentKind.description: How an attachment is supplied to the workflow copilot.

WorkflowCallRequest.description:
`POST /v1/workflows/{workflowId}:call` request. The same body as
`runs.create` plus an optional `waitMs` wait bound: this custom method both
creates a run and waits for it to finish, returning the run and its output
in a single response.

idempotencyKey.description:
Replay-safe key: an identical `(workflow, idempotencyKey)` returns the
existing run instead of starting a new one.

input.description: Payload delivered to the trigger node (default `{}`).

only.description: Execute exactly this set of node ids; all others are skipped.

seedOutputs.description: Inject outputs for nodes treated as already-produced.

startAt.description: Execute only this node and its transitive descendants.

usePinned.description: When true, any node with `pinnedOutput` set returns it verbatim.

waitMs.description:
Max milliseconds to wait for the run to finish before returning it still
running. Bounded by the workflow's `settings.deadlineMs`; when omitted,
a proxy-safe default (30s) is used so the call returns promptly. The run
keeps executing in the background either way — poll `runs.get` to finish.

WorkflowCallResponse.description:
`POST /v1/workflows/{workflowId}:call` response: the full run, its final
output, and the observed status. When the wait bound elapses before the run
finishes, `status` is `running`, `output` is null, and `hint` points at
`runs.get` for polling — the run continues in the background.

hint.description: Present only when `status` is `running`: how to poll for completion.

output.description: Final run output (sink node outputs) when the run finished; else null.

WorkflowComposeChoice.description:
One safe, user-facing answer to a workflow setup question. Selecting a
choice sends its bounded follow-up prompt through the same non-mutating
composition path; the browser never writes configuration directly.

WorkflowComposeMessage.description:
One bounded turn of workflow-authoring context. The draft remains the
source of truth; history only helps the copilot interpret follow-up edits.

WorkflowComposeQuestion.description: A setup question Architect must resolve before proposing a workflow.

WorkflowComposeRequest.description:
Ask the workflow copilot to search the SDK or propose a complete draft.
The request never saves or executes the workflow.

attachments.description: Ephemeral text, image, or file-reference context. Never persisted.

history.description: Recent bounded turns for follow-up requests such as “send that weekly”.

model.description: Optional entitled model id. The server-side provider default is used when omitted.

WorkflowComposeResponse.description: A non-mutating workflow proposal or full-catalog search result.

questions.description: Setup choices that must be answered before a safe proposal can be made.

valid.description: Whether the proposal passed validation. Absent for catalog-only results.

edges.description: Directed edges carrying data between nodes.

id.description: Workflow id; doubles as the resource id (`[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,64}`).

deadlineMs.description: Wall-clock budget for a whole run (default 300000, max 3600000).

maxOutputBytes.description:
Per-node stored-output cap; larger outputs are truncated in the record
(execution still sees full values). Default 262144, max 2097152.

tags.name: runs
tags.description: Deterministic run execution: job pattern, per-node states, SSE events

tags.name: nodeTypes
tags.description: Data-driven node catalog for the studio palette
